title: Smart Plant Monitor
summary: Track soil moisture and trigger watering alerts before your plants dry out.
overview: This project samples soil moisture every 15 minutes, logs the readings, and alerts you when the plant needs water. Add a relay module later to automate a pump.

steps.title: Prepare the Pi and enable GPIO
steps.detail: Update Raspberry Pi OS, install Python dependencies, and verify GPIO access with a simple blink test.

steps.title: Wire the moisture sensor
steps.detail: Connect VCC, GND, and output signal to a safe GPIO input path using the resistor network from your kit.

steps.title: Calibrate dry and wet thresholds
steps.detail: Record analog-like readings in dry air and fully wet soil so your alert thresholds reflect your actual sensor.

steps.title: Run continuous sampler
steps.detail: Schedule a loop that logs moisture percentage and stores data to CSV for trend analysis.

steps.title: Add alerts
steps.detail: Send terminal alerts (or optional webhook notifications) when moisture drops below your threshold for two consecutive checks.

codeExample:
import time
from gpiozero import DigitalInputDevice

MOISTURE_PIN = 17
sensor = DigitalInputDevice(MOISTURE_PIN, pull_up=True)

DRY_ALERT_SECONDS = 60
dry_since = None

while True:
    is_dry = sensor.value == 1
    now = time.time()

    if is_dry:
        dry_since = dry_since or now
        if now - dry_since > DRY_ALERT_SECONDS:
            print("Soil is dry. Time to water your plant.")
    else:
        dry_since = None
        print("Moisture level looks healthy.")

    time.sleep(5)

wiring.notes: Capacitive moisture sensors are more reliable than resistive probes and corrode less over time.

deliverables: Moisture alert script running on boot

title: Room Climate Dashboard
summary: Build a live indoor climate dashboard with temperature, humidity, and pressure trends.
overview: Create a compact desk dashboard that polls the BME280 every few seconds and displays key readings on an OLED panel.

steps.title: Enable I2C on Raspberry Pi
steps.detail: Use raspi-config to enable I2C and verify the BME280 appears with i2cdetect.

steps.title: Wire BME280 and OLED
steps.detail: Share SDA/SCL lines and connect power/ground for both devices on the same I2C bus.

steps.title: Install display and sensor libraries
steps.detail: Install adafruit-circuitpython-bme280 and luma.oled for reading and rendering values.

steps.title: Render real-time dashboard
steps.detail: Display temperature, humidity, and pressure with min/max values from the current session.

steps.title: Set auto-start service
steps.detail: Create a systemd unit so the dashboard restarts automatically after reboot.

codeExample:
import time
import board
import busio
import adafruit_bme280

i2c = busio.I2C(board.SCL, board.SDA)
bme280 = adafruit_bme280.Adafruit_BME280_I2C(i2c, address=0x76)

while True:
    print(f"Temp: {bme280.temperature:.1f} C")
    print(f"Humidity: {bme280.humidity:.1f}%")
    print(f"Pressure: {bme280.pressure:.1f} hPa")
    print("---")
    time.sleep(2)

wiring.notes: Keep I2C wires short to avoid flaky reads.

deliverables: Optional long-term log file for trends

title: Ultrasonic Parking Assistant
summary: Measure car-to-wall distance and provide color LED proximity feedback.
overview: Turn your garage into a guided parking bay with visual distance zones and optional beeping alerts.

steps.title: Wire HC-SR04 with level-safe echo path
steps.detail: Use a resistor divider on ECHO before connecting to Pi GPIO to protect 3.3V logic pins.

steps.title: Build LED distance indicator
steps.detail: Assign green/yellow/red LEDs to safe/caution/stop distance ranges.

steps.title: Implement distance sampling
steps.detail: Trigger ultrasonic pulse, measure echo time, and convert to centimeters.

steps.title: Tune distance thresholds
steps.detail: Adjust trigger points to match your garage and car dimensions.

steps.title: Mount and test
steps.detail: Position sensor at bumper height and run parking trials for consistent readings.

codeExample:
import time
from gpiozero import DistanceSensor, LED

sensor = DistanceSensor(echo=24, trigger=23, max_distance=3)
led_green = LED(5)
led_yellow = LED(6)
led_red = LED(13)

while True:
    distance_cm = sensor.distance * 100

    led_green.off(); led_yellow.off(); led_red.off()

    if distance_cm > 120:
        led_green.on()
    elif distance_cm > 60:
        led_yellow.on()
    else:
        led_red.on()

    print(f"Distance: {distance_cm:.1f} cm")
    time.sleep(0.2)

wiring.notes: Add a 1k/2k voltage divider on ECHO line to keep Pi input safe.

title: Motion Alert Camera
summary: Capture images when motion is detected and keep timestamped evidence clips.
overview: Pair a PIR sensor with the Pi camera to create a low-cost smart security watcher for entryways or garages.

steps.title: Attach and test camera
steps.detail: Enable camera interface and verify still capture with libcamera-still.

steps.title: Wire PIR sensor
steps.detail: Connect PIR output to GPIO input and validate state changes with a quick test script.

steps.title: Capture on trigger
steps.detail: Create a script that saves timestamped image bursts when motion is detected.

steps.title: Add cooldown and retention policy
steps.detail: Avoid repeated triggers every second and auto-delete old files to conserve space.

steps.title: Harden service for 24/7 run
steps.detail: Create a systemd service with restart policy and log rotation.

codeExample:
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from gpiozero import MotionSensor
from picamera2 import Picamera2
import time

out_dir = Path("captures")
out_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

pir = MotionSensor(18)
camera = Picamera2()
camera.configure(camera.create_still_configuration())
camera.start()

last_capture = 0
COOLDOWN = 10

while True:
    if pir.motion_detected and time.time() - last_capture > COOLDOWN:
        stamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
        file_path = out_dir / f"motion_{stamp}.jpg"
        camera.capture_file(str(file_path))
        print(f"Captured {file_path}")
        last_capture = time.time()

    time.sleep(0.2)

wiring.notes: Allow PIR sensor to warm up for 30-60 seconds before relying on detections.

title: Voice-Controlled Lighting
summary: Turn a desk lamp on/off with offline voice commands.
overview: Use offline speech recognition to trigger relay-based lamp control without relying on cloud APIs.

steps.title: Configure microphone and audio output
steps.detail: Set default ALSA input/output devices and test with arecord and aplay.

steps.title: Wire relay control channel
steps.detail: Connect relay IN pin to GPIO and verify relay toggles using a basic Python script.

steps.title: Install speech recognition runtime
steps.detail: Install Vosk and load a compact offline model optimized for command recognition.

steps.title: Map commands to actions
steps.detail: Support phrases like "lights on" and "lights off" with confidence threshold checks.

steps.title: Add failsafe timeout
steps.detail: Ensure relay defaults to off after reboot or script crash to avoid unsafe behavior.

codeExample:
import json
from gpiozero import OutputDevice

relay = OutputDevice(22, active_high=False, initial_value=False)

def handle_command(text: str) -> None:
    cleaned = text.lower().strip()
    if "lights on" in cleaned:
        relay.on()
        print("Lamp switched on")
    elif "lights off" in cleaned:
        relay.off()
        print("Lamp switched off")

# Example transcript events from speech recognizer
for payload in ["{\"text\": \"lights on\"}", "{\"text\": \"lights off\"}"]:
    text = json.loads(payload)["text"]
    handle_command(text)

wiring.notes: Only switch properly isolated mains-rated loads through certified relay hardware.

title: Smart Doorbell Monitor
summary: Capture a snapshot and play a custom chime whenever the doorbell button is pressed.
overview: Create a modern doorbell workflow with image capture, timestamp logging, and local sound feedback.

steps.title: Wire the button with pull-down resistor
steps.detail: Use a stable GPIO input circuit to avoid accidental triggers.

steps.title: Capture image on press
steps.detail: Connect button interrupt to camera snapshot logic and save files by date folder.

steps.title: Play local chime
steps.detail: Trigger a WAV file playback through your powered speaker when events occur.

steps.title: Record event log
steps.detail: Save button presses with timestamps and image filenames for quick review.

codeExample:
from datetime import datetime
from gpiozero import Button

button = Button(25, pull_up=False)

def on_press() -> None:
    stamp = datetime.now().isoformat(timespec="seconds")
    print(f"Doorbell pressed at {stamp}")
    # capture_image(stamp)
    # play_chime()

button.when_pressed = on_press
print("Doorbell monitor armed")
button.wait_for_press()

wiring.notes: Use debouncing in software to prevent duplicate logs from noisy mechanical buttons.

title: Timelapse Wildlife Station
summary: Build an outdoor timelapse station that shoots on schedule and motion events.
overview: Combine scheduled captures with motion-triggered bursts to monitor bird feeders, garden beds, or wildlife trails.

steps.title: Install weather-safe enclosure
steps.detail: Mount the camera and sensor in a splash-resistant enclosure with clear lens window.

steps.title: Sync RTC fallback clock
steps.detail: Configure DS3231 as fallback time source for reliable scheduling without internet.

steps.title: Implement capture scheduler
steps.detail: Shoot stills every fixed interval and append motion-event bursts when PIR triggers.

steps.title: Add storage retention rules
steps.detail: Keep full-resolution recent files and downsample or archive older captures.

steps.title: Generate daily timelapse
steps.detail: Batch images into a compressed video with ffmpeg as overnight maintenance task.

codeExample:
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path

capture_root = Path("wildlife")
capture_root.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

def capture_frame(tag: str) -> Path:
    stamp = datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    file_path = capture_root / f"{tag}_{stamp}.jpg"
    # camera.capture_file(file_path)
    print(f"Captured {file_path}")
    return file_path

capture_frame("scheduled")

wiring.notes: If deployed outdoors, include surge protection and strain relief on cables.

title: LED Music Visualizer
summary: Sync NeoPixel animations to live audio with beat-sensitive color transitions.
overview: Capture live audio amplitude and frequency bands, then render responsive visual effects across addressable LEDs.

steps.title: Wire NeoPixel strip with data resistor
steps.detail: Add inline resistor on data line and common ground between Pi and LED power source.

steps.title: Set up audio stream
steps.detail: Read microphone frames with PyAudio and compute RMS/FFT features.

steps.title: Map sound energy to colors
steps.detail: Use low/mid/high band levels to drive gradient palettes and pulse intensity.

steps.title: Tune smoothing and decay
steps.detail: Apply rolling averages to reduce flicker and improve visual quality.

steps.title: Profile CPU usage
steps.detail: Balance frame rate and FFT size for stable real-time behavior on your Pi model.

wiring.notes: Power larger NeoPixel strips from a dedicated 5V supply and share ground with the Pi.

title: Retro Console Launcher
summary: Turn your Pi into a plug-and-play retro gaming launcher with physical controls.
overview: Create a custom launcher control panel that boots into your emulator frontend and supports physical start/reset buttons.

steps.title: Flash RetroPie or Batocera
steps.detail: Install a gaming image to your high-endurance microSD card and complete first boot setup.

steps.title: Wire start and reset buttons
steps.detail: Connect buttons to GPIO with proper pull-down resistors and verify input stability.

steps.title: Map button actions
steps.detail: Use a Python daemon to send launcher events such as quick-start game list or safe reset.

steps.title: Polish startup experience
steps.detail: Add branded splash screen and auto-launch behavior for living-room-friendly use.

codeExample:
from gpiozero import Button

start_button = Button(20, pull_up=False)
reset_button = Button(21, pull_up=False)

def on_start() -> None:
    print("Start button pressed: opening favorites list")

def on_reset() -> None:
    print("Reset button pressed: requesting safe reboot")

start_button.when_pressed = on_start
reset_button.when_pressed = on_reset

print("Retro launcher controls active")
start_button.wait_for_press()

wiring.notes: Use enclosure-mounted arcade buttons for a durable console feel.
